summary: Empty commit to nudge a staging deploy.
details: No code change. Pushed solely to trigger the Vercel auto-deploy for the staging branch when CI/CD investigation is in progress.
userImpact: No user-visible change — internal deploy verification.
bullets: Empty commit to trigger staging deploy
No application code modified

summary: Icon rail in the sidebar now scrolls vertically when icons exceed the viewport height.
details: First pass at making the rail handle overflow on shorter viewports — adds a hidden vertical scrollbar to the rail group so all nav icons stay reachable. The polished version with pinned header/footer and a fade mask shipped in a later commit (`01409b2`).
userImpact: On smaller laptop screens, no nav icons get cut off — you can scroll through them all.
bullets: Rail group scrolls when items overflow
Hidden scrollbar preserves chrome-free look

summary: Sidebar can now be collapsed via a chevron toggle in the rail; preference persists across sessions.
details: Adds a collapse/expand chevron button to the icon rail. The context panel slides closed with a 200ms transition, and the choice is saved in localStorage so the next page load opens in the same state. Defaults to expanded on first visit.
userImpact: More screen real estate for the main content area when you don't need the secondary nav. Setting sticks across visits.
bullets: Chevron toggle collapses the sidebar context panel
Smooth 200ms transition
State persisted to localStorage
Defaults to expanded for new visitors

summary: Per-role dashboards, supervisor hub with summary cards, workload matrix with reassignment recommendations, and three new nav items (Supervisor, Coaching, Drafts).
details:
Final synthesis layer that ties every supervisor sub-module together into a navigable admin surface and wires per-role metrics into each persona's dashboard.

**Workload matrix (SM-1).** New `getWorkloadMatrix` action returns every user in the org with open/overdue task counts, active case count, and last activity. The `/admin/supervisor` page pivots this with role/team filters, sortable by overdue count, with users running 2× the team average highlighted.

**Imbalance detection + reassignment (SM-4).** `workload-imbalance.ts` uses z-score outlier detection to flag overloaded and underutilized users per role. `recommendReassignments` pairs each overloaded user with an underutilized teammate and selects the most-overdue task to move. The `/admin/supervisor/workload` page shows per-role imbalances with admin-only Apply buttons that call the existing `reassignTask` action.

**Per-role metric dashboards (SM-5).** The `/dashboard` route now renders the full metric pack (`getRoleMetricPack(persona)`) instead of just the primary KPI. Each card shows current value, target, band color (via `evaluateMetric`), and an inline 7-day sparkline from `performanceSnapshots`. A composite score headline sits at the top via `computeCompositeScore`. Try/catch wrapping means missing snapshot data renders placeholders instead of crashing.

**Supervisor hub.** `/admin/supervisor` shows six summary cards: open supervisor events, open coaching flags, open compliance findings, high/critical risk cases, pending AI drafts, and the workload matrix. Gated to admin and reviewer personas. A dedicated `/admin/supervisor/drafts` page provides the org-wide AI draft inbox.

**Nav.** Three new rail items: supervisor, coaching, drafts. Custom SVG icons; registered in the shared `nav-items.ts` registry; added to the admin and reviewer persona nav arrays.

**Sparkline helper.** New `components/charts/sparkline.tsx` — minimal 80×24 inline-SVG sparkline with empty/single-point fallbacks.

**Cron schedule.** Seven new crons added to `vercel.json` (later downgraded for Hobby compliance): performance-rollup, compliance-scan, coaching-scan, stagnant-scan, deadline-scan, risk-scan, escalate-overdue.
userImpact: Admins and reviewers finally have a unified hub showing what's broken, who's overloaded, and what's pending across every supervisor workflow. Each role's dashboard now reflects the metrics they actually care about, with sparklines showing trend.
riskNotes: Cron schedules added here used sub-daily frequencies later downgraded for Vercel Hobby. Reassignment Apply buttons mutate task ownership — admin-only gating is the only safety net.
bullets: Workload matrix with overload/underutilization detection
Admin-applied reassignment recommendations
Per-role dashboards with metric packs + sparklines
Supervisor hub with 6 status summary cards
3 new nav items (supervisor, coaching, drafts)

summary: Coaching workflow, heuristic case risk scoring, and a 4-rule compliance scanner — three new daily crons feed all of it.
details:
Three modules covering coaching (CC-1 through CC-4), case risk (PR-1), and compliance monitoring (PR-2).

**Coaching workflow.** `coaching-detection.ts` reads each user's last 7 days of `performanceSnapshots`, evaluates every metric, and raises a `coaching_flags` row when a user has ≥2 critical-band metrics. `findOutliers` classifies the issue as 'people' (this user) vs 'process' (the whole role is below). Auto-notifies the user's supervisor (or org admin as fallback). `detectTrainingGaps` raises `training_gaps` rows when ≥50% of a role is below the warn threshold for a metric. `coaching-draft.ts` pulls the user's 14-day activity (tasks + comms + transitions + audit log) and asks Claude to draft both a coaching conversation and a phone call script. The `/coaching` index lists open flags; `/coaching/[flagId]` has conversation + call-script tabs with Generate, Resolve, and Dismiss actions. `/coaching/training-gaps` lists gaps grouped by role. Cron runs at 4am UTC.

**Case risk scoring (PR-1, heuristic).** `risk-scorer.ts` computes a 0-100 score from seven weighted factors: stage dwell time (18), overdue tasks (14), ALJ historical win rate (12), communication gap (14), MR completeness (10), hearing-proximity-with-incomplete-PHI combo (22), client sentiment labels (10). Bands: low/medium/high/critical. Factors persisted as JSON so the UI can show 'why this case is flagged' without re-running. Cases list shows a colored risk pill next to the stage; `/reports/risk` renders a grid of high/critical cases with their top 3 contributing factors. Cron runs hourly at :15.

**Compliance monitoring (PR-2).** `compliance-scanner.ts` ships four real rules (not stubs): HIPAA_PHI_CREDENTIAL_STORAGE (provider creds without encrypted password or unrotated >1 year), BAR_CASE_STAGE_STAGNANT_90D (active cases with no transition in 90 days), BAR_MISSING_WELCOME_CALL (intake without `phone_outbound` within 7 days), ETHICS_MISSING_FEE_AGREEMENT (active cases without a fee_agreement document). Findings deduped by (rule, subject). `/admin/compliance` has Open / Rules / All findings tabs with ack/remediate/false-positive actions. Cron runs daily at 3:30am UTC.
userImpact: Supervisors get a coaching queue showing exactly which staff need attention and which whole-role processes are weak. Cases-list users see at-a-glance risk pills. Admins get a compliance findings dashboard backed by real rules instead of placeholder logic.
riskNotes: Risk scorer is heuristic — early scores will need calibration once you see real distributions. Compliance rule false-positive rates are unknown until run against production data.
bullets: Coaching detection + draft generation + UI
Heuristic risk scorer with 7 weighted signals
4 real compliance rules (HIPAA, bar, ethics)
3 new daily crons: coaching, risk, compliance

summary: QA layer adds sentiment classification on inbound messages, AI review on outbound messages, and the call-transcript pipeline scaffold.
details:
Three QA stories shipping together.

**Sentiment analysis (QA-3).** `sentiment.ts` classifies every inbound message via Claude into positive / neutral / confused / frustrated / angry / churn_risk and updates the `communications` row's sentiment columns. Fires via `after()` from the case-status webhook on inbound message receipt. `case-health.ts` aggregates recent sentiment into a 0-100 case-health signal with trend direction (uses `classifyTrend`). The `/admin/qa/client-health` dashboard sorts worst-first with red/amber/green bands. Cases list shows an 'At risk' red pill when the most recent communication is frustrated/angry/churn_risk.

**Outbound message QA (QA-2).** `message-qa.ts` calls Claude with a review prompt that returns `{passed, score, issues[], suggestions[]}` and updates the communication row's `qaStatus`/`qaScore`/`qaNotes`. Fires via `after()` from the outbound send path — does not block the send. The `/admin/qa/messages` dashboard offers score filtering and per-sender 30-day average.

**Call transcript pipeline (QA-1, scaffold).** Schema (`callRecordings`, `callTranscripts`, `callQcReviews`) was already in place. Adds an HMAC-verified webhook at `/api/webhooks/calltools/route.ts` that inserts a recording row with status `pending_transcription` and schedules transcription via `after()`. Transcription itself is a stub that writes a placeholder transcript with `provider='stub'` — the file's top-of-comment documents exact Deepgram + Whisper integration steps. The QC reviewer (`call-qc.ts`) is real Claude-backed with quality/compliance/empathy/professionalism sub-scores. The `/admin/qa/calls` viewer shows speaker segments, scores sidebar, flags, highlights, and a stub-warning banner when the provider is 'stub'.
userImpact: QA team can monitor client sentiment trends, catch problematic outbound messages before they become support fires, and (once Deepgram is wired) review actual call transcripts with AI-scored quality. Cases that are going off the rails get a visible 'At risk' badge on the cases list.
riskNotes: Call transcription is intentionally stubbed — actual transcripts won't be real until DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is set (shipped in a later commit, `62376d6`).
bullets: Inbound sentiment classification (6 labels)
AI review on every outbound message
Case-health signal with red/amber/green bands
'At risk' pill on the cases list
Call transcript pipeline scaffold (Deepgram-ready)

summary: Reports module: nightly performance rollups, role leaderboards, per-user trends, team handoff matrix, and bottleneck analysis.
details:
Per-user and per-team performance reporting driven by a nightly cron rolling raw activity into `performance_snapshots` and `team_performance_snapshots` tables. Result: dashboards become O(1) lookups instead of re-aggregating raw communications/tasks/transitions on every page load.

**Metric collectors.** 28 fully-implemented collectors reading from real tables: task completion, response time, unread backlog, active cases, stage transitions per week, stagnant count, follow-up compliance, new leads, conversion rate, contracts sent, applications filed, ready→filed turnaround, queue depth, MR requests sent, MR turnaround, RFC forms, PHI sheets completed, PHI turnaround, PHI overdue, hearings this week, win rate, prep completion, avg case age, hearings represented, transcript QC avg, mail items processed, ERE credentials. Stubbed collectors return 0 with a context note for metrics whose tables aren't on this branch yet.

**Nightly rollup cron** (`performance-rollup`, 2am UTC). Upserts per-user snapshots by `(userId, metricKey, periodStart)` and team-level snapshots by `(team, metricKey, periodStart)`. Runtime capped at 3 minutes; per-user errors logged without failing the run.

**Pages.**
- `/reports/team-performance` — org index grouped by role.
- `/reports/team-performance/[userId]` — per-user drill-down with inline SVG sparklines.
- `/reports/leaderboards` — role/metric/period picker with composite and single-metric tables.
- `/reports/handoffs` — team × team handoff matrix + slowest-handoff list.
- `/reports/bottlenecks` — stage list with 'why' heuristic.

**Seed.** `seed-performance-snapshots.ts` ran a 7-day backfill on staging — 231 user snapshots across 8 users × 24 metrics, plus 189 team snapshots. So leaderboards and trend charts render real data on first load.
userImpact: Managers can rank teammates on any metric, see per-user 7-day trends with sparklines, identify the slowest team-to-team handoffs, and find which case stages bottleneck most often. All backed by a nightly rollup so report pages load instantly.
riskNotes: Performance snapshots accumulate over time — long-term storage growth should be monitored. Some collectors are stubbed where tables don't exist yet; check the collector source if a metric reads zero unexpectedly.
bullets: 28 real metric collectors querying actual tables
Nightly rollup cron at 2am UTC
Leaderboards + per-user trends + handoff matrix
Bottleneck analysis with stage-level 'why'
7-day backfill seeded on staging

summary: Supervisor event bus drives proactive case action — webhooks fire events that route to AI drafts, assigned tasks, escalations, and notifications.
details:
Establishes the central nervous system for AI-driven case action. Events fire from webhooks and background scans, get routed to the right AI draft generator, assigned to the responsible role, escalated when not acted on, and tracked through detected → draft_created → task_assigned → resolved.

**Notifications bell + inbox.** Server actions in `notifications.ts`; client component `notification-bell.tsx` with 30-second unread polling, dropdown panel, mark-all-read, priority badges, relative time. Wired into the rail. Dates serialized to ISO across the client boundary.

**Supervisor timeline (SA-8).** New `/cases/[id]/supervisor-timeline` page rendering each event as a card with severity badge, summary, recommendedAction, status pill, vertical step timeline, and linked tasks/drafts/notifications. Inline resolve/dismiss actions.

**Event-driven AI drafts (SA-2, SA-3).** `event-drafts.ts` provides per-event-type generators: appeal reconsideration, AC brief, pre-hearing brief, fee petition, client denial notification, hearing notification. Each uses `buildCaseContext` and inserts an `ai_drafts` row linked to the source event. `event-router.ts:handleSupervisorEvent(eventId)` maps event types to the right draft bundle and advances the lifecycle.

**Webhook event detection.** `app/api/webhooks/ere/route.ts` snapshots prior case state, detects new hearing dates and decisions, records a `supervisor_events` row, and fires `handleSupervisorEvent` + `executeEventWorkflows` via `after()`.

**SSA-deadline-aware task assignment (SA-5).** New `executeEventWorkflows` in `workflow-engine.ts`. Loads `event_detected` workflow templates, matches by `triggerConfig.eventType`, computes `dueDates` via `getDeadlineRuleForEvent` + `computeDeadlineDate`, resolves assignee from the deadline rule's `ownerRole`, stamps `source_event_id`.

**Auto-populate contacts from extraction (SA-6).** `contact-autolink.ts:autoLinkContactsFromExtraction` reads `documentProcessingResults.aiClassification.extractions`, upserts provider/ssa_judge/representative contacts, links via `caseContacts`. Document processor calls it after `createChronologyEntry`.

**3-tier escalation ladder (SA-7).** `app/api/cron/escalate-overdue/route.ts` runs every 30 minutes. Walks overdue tasks: reminder to assignee → supervisor notification at 1 day → admin blast at 2 days. Cap 200 per run.

**Client task confirmation (CM-3).** `message-intake.ts:extractActionItemsFromMessage` extracts action items with Claude and creates `pending_client_confirmation` tasks plus an outbound message asking the client. `classifyClientConfirmationReply` classifies responses as yes/no/unclear and updates the task.

**Stagnant + deadline scan crons.** `stagnant-scan` (every 4h) flags cases with no activity in 14+ days; `deadline-scan` (hourly) flags approaching SSA deadlines.
userImpact: When SSA events come in (new hearing date, new decision, etc.), the system automatically drafts the right paperwork, assigns the right tasks with proper deadlines, and notifies the right people. Overdue tasks escalate up the chain on a clock. Inbound client messages spawn confirmation tasks automatically.
riskNotes: Lots of automated actions firing without human approval. Watch the supervisor timeline closely after enabling on a new case load.
bullets: Notifications bell with 30s polling
Event-driven AI drafts on hearing/decision/deadline events
SSA-deadline-aware auto task assignment
Auto-link providers/judges from document extraction
3-tier escalation ladder for overdue tasks
Client message → confirmation task flow

summary: Full AI-powered client communication module — case-aware drafts, per-artifact generators, auto-logged activity, and a reviewer inbox.
details:
Five sub-stories shipping together.

**CM-2 — Case-specific draft replies (35 → 85).** `draftCommunication` in `app/actions/ai.ts` rewritten to call `buildCaseContext + formatCaseContextForPrompt`. Returns `{draft, disclaimer, contextSignals[]}` so reviewers see what context the AI used. New `draftReplyToMessage(communicationId)` persists an `ai_drafts` row with `type=client_message`, `sourceCommunicationId`, `assignedReviewerId` and a `generating → draft_ready/error` lifecycle. Notifies the assigned reviewer when ready. Inbound messages get a 'Draft AI reply' button with inline edit / approve-and-send / reject controls. Edit distance is tracked for AI-quality measurement.

**CM-4 — Per-artifact generators (18 → 80).** `lib/services/ai-drafts.ts` adds: `draftMedicalRecordsRequest`, `draftClientLetter`, `draftCallScript`, `draftTaskInstructions`, `draftRfcLetter`, `draftStatusUpdate`. Server action wrappers + `updateDraftBody`/`approveDraft`/`rejectDraft`. Task detail page gets a 'Generate AI draft' dropdown plus a per-task 'AI help' button. New `/drafts` reviewer inbox lists pending drafts with filter toggles; `/drafts/[id]` has an editable body, approve flow that inserts the final artifact (communications or documents row depending on type), and reject action.

**CM-5 — Auto-logged activity timeline (45 → 90).** New `logCommunicationEvent` and `logAiDraftEvent` helpers in `hipaa-audit.ts`. Inbound and outbound communications now write audit rows. Case activity page reads both communications and ai_drafts alongside stage transitions and workflow events, ordered by timestamp. Timeline component gets `ai_draft_*` event styling.
userImpact: Every client-facing email and message can be drafted by AI in seconds with full case context. Reviewers approve/edit/reject from a dedicated inbox. Every draft generated, edited, approved, or sent shows up in the case activity timeline so there's a complete audit trail.
riskNotes: Approving a draft is a one-click send — make sure reviewer access is gated correctly to prevent accidental client communications.
bullets: Case-aware AI replies with context signals shown to reviewer
6 per-artifact generators (MR request, letter, call script, etc.)
Reviewer inbox at /drafts with filter + approve/reject
Edit distance tracked for AI-quality measurement
Full draft lifecycle visible in case activity timeline

summary: Six cross-cutting service primitives that the rest of the supervisor module builds on.
details:
Foundation library that every AI/automation path consumes. Avoids ad-hoc reimplementations.

**case-context.ts** — `buildCaseContext(caseId)` returns a structured bundle: case meta, claimant, stage history, communications, medical chronology, documents, open tasks. Single source of truth for 'what does the AI need to know about this case?'. `formatCaseContextForPrompt(ctx)` serializes to a prompt-ready blob; callers wanting structured access slice the bundle directly.

**notify.ts** — `createNotification`, `markNotificationRead`, `markAllNotificationsRead`, `getUserNotifications`, `getUnreadNotificationCount`. Dedupe via `dedupeKey` so repeated scans don't spam. In-app delivery is real; email/SMS/push are stubbed delivery rows for later wiring (real delivery shipped in `04f1eeb`).

**ssa-deadlines.ts** — `SSA_DEADLINE_RULES` with rules for reconsideration, hearing, AC, federal court, five-day evidence, fee petition, good-cause response, RFC follow-up, MR follow-up. Helpers: `computeDeadlineDate`, `computeWarningDates`, `getDeadlineRuleForEvent`, `daysUntilDeadline`, `currentWarningLevel`. Weekend rollover support per rule.

**role-metrics.ts** — `ROLE_METRICS` dict with per-role metric packs for 14 roles (intake_agent through admin). Each metric defines label, unit, direction, target, warn/critical thresholds, weight. `evaluateMetric()` returns `null | 'warn' | 'critical'`. `computeCompositeScore()` produces a 0-100 weighted score per user.

**pattern-analysis.ts** — Pure stats library (no DB imports) so CLI scripts can consume. `computeStats`, `zScore`, `findOutliers`, `classifyProblem` (for RP-3 process-vs-people detection), `computeDelta`, `classifyTrend`.

**supervisor-events.ts** — `recordSupervisorEvent`, `advanceSupervisorEvent`, `linkArtifactToEvent`, `getSupervisorEventsForCase`, `getOpenSupervisorEventsForUser`. Lifecycle steps appended to JSONB on each transition.
userImpact: Pure foundation. No direct UI but every smart feature in the supervisor module relies on these — case-aware AI drafts, role-based metrics, SSA deadline awareness, outlier detection.
bullets: buildCaseContext: single source of truth for AI prompts
Notification primitives with dedupe
9 SSA deadline rules with weekend rollover
Per-role metric packs + composite scoring
Pure-stats helpers (z-scores, outliers, trends)

summary: Schema foundation for the AI Supervisor + Communication modules — 8 new tables, 11 new enums, and extensions to communications and tasks.
details:
Migration `0008_calm_krista_starr.sql` (already applied to staging). Pure schema commit setting up everything the next ten commits build on.

**New tables.**
- `notifications`, `notification_deliveries`, `notification_preferences` (feed SA-1, SA-7, coaching alerts)
- `supervisor_events` (unified event log with lifecycle steps JSONB, linked artifacts, recommended actions — feeds SA-1, SA-5, SA-8)
- `ai_drafts` (reviewable AI letters, briefs, scripts, messages — feeds CM-2, CM-4, SA-2, SA-3, SA-4)
- `performance_snapshots`, `team_performance_snapshots` (daily rollups by user/team/metric — feeds RP-1, RP-2, RP-4, QA-4)
- `case_risk_scores` (heuristic risk with factor breakdown — PR-1)
- `compliance_rules`, `compliance_findings` (rule engine — PR-2)
- `call_recordings`, `call_transcripts`, `call_qc_reviews` (transcript ingest + QC — QA-1)
- `coaching_flags`, `coaching_drafts`, `training_gaps` (coaching workflow — CC-1 through CC-4)

**Enum expansions.** `user_role`: +fee_collection, hearing_advocate, appeals_council, post_hearing, pre_hearing_prep. `task_status`: +pending_client_confirmation. `workflow_trigger_type`: +message_received, event_detected. Plus 11 new enums (supervisor_event_type, sentiment_label, ai_draft_type, escalation_state, etc.).

**Existing-table extensions.** `communications` adds threadId, readAt, deliveryStatus, deliveredAt, respondedAt, responseTimeSeconds, respondedBy, sentimentScore/Label/AnalyzedAt, qaStatus/Score/Notes/ReviewedAt + a `uq_comms_external` unique index. `tasks` adds sourceCommunicationId, sourceEventId, escalationState, lastEscalatedAt, clientConfirmation* fields.
userImpact: Pure schema. Sets the stage for all the AI supervisor features that follow.
riskNotes: Migration named 0008 to avoid colliding with the search foundation migrations 0006/0007. If applied out of order with other branches, expect conflicts.
bullets: Migration 0008 (already applied to staging)
8 new tables for events, drafts, snapshots, risk, compliance, calls, coaching
11 new enums
communications + tasks extended with metadata for AI workflows
5 new user_role values

summary: Strategic planning docs: the 29-story AI Supervisor build plan and the process-elimination framework for role headcount analysis.
details:
Two docs prompted by Austin Hogan's three emails on 2026-04-11 describing the AI client communication module, the AI supervisor module, and the broader process-elimination discussion.

**ai-supervisor-plan.md** parses the emails into 29 user stories grouped into 7 capability areas: CM (communication), QA (quality), SM (monitoring), SA (proactive action), RP (reporting), CC (coaching), PR (predictive). Establishes a baseline scoring average of 23/100 across all stories — strong data layer, very thin intelligence layer on top. Identifies 15 cross-cutting building blocks (the biggest: `buildCaseContext`, notifications subsystem, supervisor event bus, generalized workflow triggers, performance snapshots, role metric dictionary, SSA deadline engine). Tiered plan: Tier 1 foundation → Tier 2 quick wins → Tier 3 big rocks → Tier 4 heavy greenfield. Honest scope notes that 'all tiers to 100' ships data model + actions + UI for every story while stubbing external service integrations with clear extension points.

**process-elimination-framework.md** introduces a five-lens test (Judgment Load, Liability Exposure, Error Recoverability, Training Data Density, Client Preference) for deciding which roles to eliminate, reduce, keep human, or hybridize. Maps role-by-role analysis for all 10 Hogan Smith roles with projected headcount deltas (aggregate ~45% reduction across the current ~140 staff). Explicitly maps which Favorble features must ship before each role reduction.
userImpact: These docs are how every subsequent AI Supervisor commit is justified and prioritized. Anyone reading the changelog can see the master plan that this work is executing against.
riskNotes: Headcount projections are sensitive — keep `process-elimination-framework.md` access-controlled if it's shared outside the founding team.
bullets: 29-story AI Supervisor build plan with baseline scores
15 cross-cutting building blocks identified
Tiered roadmap (foundation → quick wins → big rocks → greenfield)
Process-elimination 5-lens framework
Role-by-role disposition + headcount delta analysis

summary: Search subsystem documentation, env reference, and a deprecation banner on the old global-search component.
details:
Makes the search work (phases 0-3) discoverable to engineers joining the project. All knowledge from the swarm research + inventory is now in the repo instead of only in chat history.

**docs/search/.**
- `README.md` — entry point with invariants and file map
- `architecture.md` — 10 design decisions with rationale
- `entity-inventory.md` — 15-entity field/facet/PHI matrix
- `runbook.md` — clone → migrate → backfill → chunk → embed → dev, plus full troubleshooting
- `roadmap.md` — phase 4 items (why deferred) + scale pivots (symptom → fix)
- `ADR-001-postgres-native.md` — options considered, decision, consequences, accepted risks

**.env.example** documents `SEARCH_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` + tuning knobs, `AZURE_OPENAI_*` (BAA-compliant default for PHI), `OPENAI_*` (fallback), `BGE_EMBEDDING_URL` (self-hosted), `RAILWAY_BUCKET_*` (already live).

**components/layout/global-search.tsx** gets a deprecation banner noting it's the old ILIKE search kept as a one-release-cycle fallback, pointing readers at the new `command-palette.tsx` and `docs/search/`.
userImpact: No runtime change. Future developers (and future-you) can find why decisions were made, how to operate the system, and what's intentionally deferred.
bullets: Full docs/search/ tree (README, architecture, runbook, roadmap, ADR)
15-entity field/facet/PHI matrix
Updated .env.example with all search-related vars
Deprecation banner on legacy global-search component

summary: Search phases 2 and 3 — semantic embedding worker plus passage-level PDF chunks with deep links to specific pages.
details:
Completes the 4-phase search plan started in the prior commit.

**Phase 2 — semantic layer.**
- `scripts/embedding-worker.ts` polls `search_documents WHERE embedding IS NULL` and fills vectors via the provider abstraction (Azure OpenAI / OpenAI / self-hosted BGE / stub). Runs as one-shot batch (`--once`) or long-lived service (`LOOP=1`). Read batch size, max chars, loop interval from env. Bumps `indexed_at` on failure for natural backoff.
- `/api/search/v2` already consumed embeddings — the worker was the missing piece. With provider=`stub` (default), semantic ranking is a no-op and the route degrades transparently to lexical.

**Phase 3 — passage-level PDF retrieval.**
- `lib/search/chunker.ts` — sentence-aware chunker. ~400-token target, ~50-token overlap, page markers via `\f` or `[[page:N]]`. Records `char_start`/`char_end`/`page_number` for every chunk. Pure function — easy to test and reuse.
- `scripts/chunk-documents.ts` reads `document_processing_results`, chunks each `extracted_text`, upserts into `document_chunks`, and mirrors every chunk as a `search_documents` row with `entity_type='document_chunk'`. Idempotent per document. Ran against staging: 30 docs → 190 chunks.
- `components/documents/document-preview.tsx` adds an `initialPage` prop, passing through to the iframe `src` via the `#page=N` PDF parameter that every browser viewer honors.
- `app/(app)/cases/[id]/documents/client.tsx` reads `?doc=<id>&page=<N>` from the URL and auto-opens the preview at that page on mount — the deep-link target for passage hits.
- `/api/search/v2` `hrefFor()` builds `/cases/{caseId}/documents?doc={docId}&page={N}` for `document_chunk` results.

Sanity-check: query 'functional capacity' returned 3 document_chunk hits from the DDE document (chunks 8, 9, 12), 1 chronology entry from an independent medical exam, and 1 chunk from an SSA HA-501 form, ranked by `ts_rank_cd`.
userImpact: Cmd+K can now find specific passages inside PDFs — clicking a result jumps directly to the matching page in the source PDF instead of just opening the document. Once a real embedding provider is configured, semantic search lights up automatically.
riskNotes: Semantic search remains dormant until `SEARCH_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` is set and the embedding worker has run a backfill.
bullets: Embedding worker for semantic search backfill
Sentence-aware chunker (~400 token chunks)
PDF deep links jump to the matching page on click
Query 'functional capacity' returns real passage hits
Lexical + passage search live; semantic dormant until provider is set

summary: Full search foundation — polymorphic search_documents table, query-parser with prefix grammar, RRF ranking, ACL filtering, and a Cmd+K command palette.
details:
Builds a 99th-percentile search experience across every entity type, backed by Postgres FTS + pgvector inside the existing Railway BAA perimeter. Zero new vendors.

**Foundation (phase 0).** `0006_search_foundation.sql` enables `pgvector` + `pg_trgm`, creates `search_documents` (polymorphic, org-scoped, ACL'd) with a generated tsvector (A=title, B=subtitle, C=body), `pgvector(1536)` embedding, jsonb facets, identifier array, per-row `allowed_roles` + `allowed_user_ids`, and `owner_user_id`. Adds GIN indexes on tsv, title trigram, identifiers, facets, plus an HNSW index on embedding (empty, populated by worker). Drizzle bindings in `db/schema/search.ts` with custom tsvector + vector(1536) types.

**Core lib.**
- `types.ts` — shared request/response shapes + per-type caps for result diversity
- `query-parser.ts` — scoped prefix grammar (`case:`, `doc:`, `@user`, `stage:4D`, `before:2024-…`), identifier detection (HS-XXXXX, SSA doc IDs, ICD-10, SSN last-4, email), date-language parser
- `rrf.ts` — Reciprocal Rank Fusion merge with per-type caps + entity-affinity boosts
- `access-filter.ts` — hard pre-filter WHERE fragment that enforces organization + allowed_roles + team-chat isolation INSIDE the query so facet counts can't leak restricted rows
- `embed-client.ts` — provider abstraction (Azure OpenAI / OpenAI / self-hosted BGE / stub) with 60s query embedding cache

**API route (`/api/search/v2`).** GET handler that parses the query, runs lexical + identifier-exact + (optional) semantic queries in parallel, performs RRF merge with per-type caps, applies entity-affinity boosts (owner_user_id = me +0.03), aggregates facets scoped to the access-filtered set, fires audit log via `after()` (errors swallowed). `ts_headline` snippets with « » → `<mark>` conversion.

**Command palette.** `command-palette.tsx` — global ⌘K modal, 640px wide, 14vh from top, debounced (120ms) fetch, grouped results by entity with per-type count badges, keyboard nav (↑↓ select, ↵ open, ⌘↵ new tab), 'matched in' sub-label, recent items in localStorage, full ARIA wiring. Zero external deps beyond what's already in the repo. Header swaps `GlobalSearch` for `CommandPalette`.

**Triggers (phase 1).** `0007_search_triggers.sql` — 13 triggers keep `search_documents` in sync synchronously inside every write transaction (cases, contacts, leads, users, documents, medical_chronology_entries, calendar_events, tasks, communications, chat_messages, outbound_mail, invoices, trust_transactions). Each trigger wraps `search_upsert()` which handles upsert + embedding reset on content change. Soft-deletes mark the row's `deleted_at`.

**Backfill.** `scripts/backfill-search-documents.ts` — no-op UPDATE per table re-fires triggers. Applied to staging: 935 rows indexed across 10 entity types.

Sanity-check: 'Williams' returned hearings + contacts + documents + tasks ranked by ts_rank_cd; 'HS-22215' returned the case row instantly.
userImpact: Cmd+K opens an instant search palette that finds anything in the system — cases, contacts, documents, tasks, messages — with smart prefix grammar (`case:`, `@user`, etc.), keyboard navigation, and exact-identifier shortcuts. Old global-search remains as a fallback for one release cycle.
riskNotes: Migrations 0006 + 0007 are large and add many indexes. Run during a low-traffic window if applying to a production-sized DB.
bullets: Polymorphic search_documents with FTS + pgvector + ACL
Cmd+K palette with prefix grammar + recent items
Per-type result caps for diversity
13 sync triggers keep search index live
935 rows indexed on staging across 10 entity types

summary: Daily cron sweeps and re-runs ingestion for any webhook documents whose pre-signed URLs expired before the original after() callback completed.
details:
Recovery layer for the rare but real case where a webhook fires, schedules ingest via `after()`, and the Lambda freezes mid-flight (transient network errors, instance recycling, source 5xx). The cron picks those up and re-runs ingest + extract.

A document qualifies when:
- `source` ∈ ('ere', 'chronicle', 'case_status')
- `storage_path` is still `http://`, `https://`, or `pending/` (anything at `railway://` was successfully ingested)
- `created_at` between 5 minutes and 7 days old (give the original a chance, stop hammering dead URLs after a week)
- `deleted_at IS NULL`

Source URL recovery order: (1) `storage_path` if it's an http(s) URL, (2) `metadata.downloadUrl` (stashed by all three webhook handlers). If neither is present, the row is marked skipped.

Idempotent because `ingestDocumentFromUrl` uses a deterministic bucket key derived from the document id. Per-run cap of 50 docs keeps the Lambda timeout predictable.

Cron config in `vercel.json` (later downgraded for Hobby plan). Auth is the standard Vercel `CRON_SECRET` Bearer pattern; in dev (NODE_ENV !== 'production') it allows unauthenticated access for local curl. Per-run summary JSON: `{ candidateCount, swept, ingested, ingestFailed, extracted, extractFailed, skipped }`.
userImpact: Webhook-delivered documents that get stuck in 'pending' state because of a network blip recover automatically within a day instead of requiring manual intervention.
riskNotes: If an external system is permanently 5xx, the cron will retry stuck docs daily for 7 days before giving up. Watch for repeated `ingestFailed` counts in cron logs.
bullets: Daily sweep finds stuck webhook docs
Recovers source URL from storage_path or metadata
Idempotent via deterministic bucket keys
50-doc cap per run for predictable runtime
Standard CRON_SECRET auth

summary: Webhook documents now download to the Railway bucket immediately so we have a durable copy before the source system's pre-signed URL expires.
details:
Webhook handlers used to store the source system's raw pre-signed download URL as `storage_path`. Those URLs typically expire in ~1 hour, which broke Reprocess, Preview, Download, and meant we had no durable copy of the file.

**New pieces.**
- `lib/services/document-ingest.ts:ingestDocumentFromUrl({...})` downloads from an http(s) URL, size-checks (50MB cap), persists to the Railway bucket at a deterministic key derived from the document id, and updates `storage_path` + `file_size_bytes` + `file_type`. Idempotent — retries overwrite the existing blob.
- `lib/storage/railway-bucket.ts` adds `uploadRailwayDocumentAtKey()` for explicit-key uploads and `buildDeterministicDocumentKey()` that embeds the document id. The existing `uploadRailwayDocument()` (timestamp-based key) is kept for manual uploads.
- `lib/services/enqueue-processing.ts:enqueueIngestAndProcessing()` runs ingest + extraction in a single `after()` block. If ingest fails (expired URL, oversize, etc.), extraction is still attempted as a best-effort fallback against the pre-existing `storage_path`.

**Storage routing updates.** `getDocumentSignedUrl` handles three new cases: http(s) paths pass through (so users can preview during the brief ingest window), `pending/` placeholders throw a 'still being downloaded' error, `chronicle://` paths still throw (caller detects).

**Webhook handlers.** `ere`, `chronicle`, and `case-status` routes all swap `enqueueDocumentProcessing` for `enqueueIngestAndProcessing` and pass `sourceUrl` from the webhook body.

Verified end-to-end against a public PDF — fetched 13KB, persisted to the Railway bucket, `storage_path` rewritten from http URL to `railway://`. Test row deleted after verification.
userImpact: Reprocess, Preview, and Download all keep working on webhook-delivered documents indefinitely instead of breaking after an hour. The firm has its own durable copy of every document instead of relying on external retention.
riskNotes: Adds storage cost as Railway bucket fills with copies of every webhook doc. Set a retention policy if needed.
bullets: Webhook docs downloaded to Railway bucket immediately
Deterministic bucket key derived from doc id (idempotent)
Best-effort fallback if ingest fails
Reprocess/Preview/Download keep working after source URL expires
Wired into ere, chronicle, and case-status webhooks

summary: Every document insertion path now auto-runs the LangExtract pipeline via Next.js after(), instead of the previously broken fire-and-forget approach.
details:
Wires the LangExtract extraction pipeline into every document insertion path. Previously only the ERE webhook attempted extraction, and even that was broken on Vercel because plain unawaited promises die when the Lambda freezes after responding.

**Shared helpers.**
- `lib/services/extraction-type.ts` — `pickExtractionTypeFromFileName` and `isExtractableDocument` (mime/extension whitelist). Pure string helpers — no server-only imports.
- `lib/services/enqueue-processing.ts:enqueueDocumentProcessing({...})` wraps `processDocument` in next/server's `after()`, which keeps the Lambda alive past the HTTP response. Skips non-extractable mime types (images, audio, video) to avoid wasting LLM tokens. Errors logged but never rethrown — a broken extractor never fails the upstream mutation.

**Wired into all 4 insertion sites.**
- `ere` webhook — replaced broken fire-and-forget with the shared helper
- `chronicle` webhook — previously never extracted; now does
- `case-status` webhook — previously never extracted client uploads; now does (filters out images/receipts automatically)
- `app/actions/documents.ts:uploadDocumentAction` (manual UI upload) — previously never extracted; attorneys upload medical records here daily, now they auto-process

Template-generated documents are intentionally NOT enqueued — they're outputs the firm generates, not inputs to extract.

**Why after() and not fire-and-forget.** On Vercel, an unawaited promise in a route handler dies the moment the function returns. `after()` is Next.js's official primitive for 'run after response, within the same execution', perfect for webhook side effects.
userImpact: Every document — whether scraped from ERE, dropped from Chronicle, uploaded by clients, or manually uploaded by attorneys — gets auto-processed by LangExtract within seconds. No more 'why didn't this medical record show up in chronology?' tickets.
riskNotes: Reprocessing webhook documents over an hour old will fail until the next commit (`6613380`) lands the Railway bucket persistence. Known follow-up flagged in the commit body.
bullets: after() wrapper keeps Lambda alive past response
Wired into all 4 document insertion paths
Mime-type whitelist skips images/audio/video
Errors logged but never break upstream mutations
Template outputs intentionally excluded

summary: Three Chronicle-related fixes — stub badges in the doc list, working extraction on railway:// paths, and recovery from a module-load-order bug.
details:
Issues surfaced by the Chronicle seed import.

**1. UX — stop silently swallowing failed previews.** `getDocumentUrl` detects `chronicle://` storage stubs and returns a structured 'metadata only' error instead of trying to sign an unresolvable URL; also wraps the signing call in try/catch so real S3 failures surface readable messages. `DocumentItem` gains `isMetadataOnly` so the list UI renders a muted 'Metadata only' badge and disables Preview/Download/Reprocess buttons on stub rows. Client handlers now show toast errors so clicks never silently no-op.

**2. Extraction — teach loadDocumentText to resolve railway:// paths.** The processor previously handled `data:` and `http(s)://` URLs and fell through to filename-only for everything else, including Railway bucket paths. So `triggerLangExtract` on a Chronicle PDF was extracting from the filename. Added a top-of-function branch: when the path starts with `railway://`, sign it via `getRailwaySignedUrl` and delegate to the http path. New `railway-pdf` / `railway-text` source labels for log clarity.

**3. Module-load-order — lazy env reads in railway-bucket.ts.** Module-level `RAILWAY_BUCKET_*` constants captured env vars at import time. Static imports hoist above any `dotenv.config()` in CLI scripts, so the constants were undefined when the module loaded and `getClient()` threw 'env vars missing' even though the values were actually set. Switched to lazy reads inside `getClient()` and each public function. Added `isRailwayBucketConfigured()` for status checks.

**Plus** `scripts/process-chronicle-pdfs.ts` (new) runs `processDocument` against all Chronicle docs with a `railway://` storage path that don't yet have completed processing results.

Ran extraction against staging: 28/30 PDFs completed; 2 failed (LangExtract returned empty — likely Gemini rate limits or low-text PDFs). Chronology entries jumped from 58 to 75 with real provider names extracted (KATAHDIN VALLEY HEALTH CENTER, CHERRY HOSPITAL, judges John Sivak and Andy Kohler).
userImpact: Chronicle-imported documents now display correctly with appropriate UI states; their PDFs actually get extracted instead of yielding garbage; the 'Reprocess' button shows real errors instead of failing silently.
bullets: 'Metadata only' badge on chronicle:// stub rows
Toast errors when getDocumentUrl fails
Extraction now works on railway:// paths (was extracting filenames before)
Lazy env-var reads fix module-load-order bug in CLI scripts
28/30 Chronicle PDFs successfully re-extracted on staging

summary: Wires the Billing, Trust, and TeamChat panels into the actual nav render block — they were defined but never displayed.
details:
The three new panel components from `f7329e1` were defined in `two-tier-nav.tsx` but never wired into the panel render switch. A parallel edit had replaced the call sites with a 'currently fall through here' comment, so the three rail items still landed on the generic `DefaultPanel` ('View all →') in the deployed staging build.

This patch renders `<BillingPanel>`, `<TrustPanel>`, `<TeamChatPanel>` right after `<MailPanel>` in the panel stack and adds the three IDs to the `DefaultPanel` exclusion list so they don't double-render.
userImpact: The Billing, Trust, and Team Chat icons in the rail now show their real money/IOLTA/mention summary panels instead of a generic 'View all →' link.
bullets: Renders BillingPanel/TrustPanel/TeamChatPanel after MailPanel
Excludes them from DefaultPanel fallback
Fixes regression from a parallel edit

summary: Real summary panels for Billing, Trust, and Team Chat — money to capture, IOLTA compliance at a glance, internal staff mentions.
details:
Fills the last three `DefaultPanel` fallthroughs with real content, finalized via a proposer swarm + critic debate.

**Billing — 'Money to capture'.** Outstanding invoice total + count (overdue count as red sublabel), unbilled billable time (hours + entries), unbilled reimbursable expenses ($ + items), draft invoices awaiting send (amber if > 0).

**Trust — 'IOLTA compliance at a glance'.** Header subline shows total balance + account count. Red dot on header when any account is negative. Pending reconciliation count, amber when > 0, red when oldest unreconciled is > 30 days. Days since last reconciled transaction (red > 30d, amber > 14d).

**Team Chat — 'Internal staff only'.** Unread @mentions (red when > 0) — strictly scoped to `chat_channels + chat_messages` joined through `chat_channel_members`, never includes client communications. Unread direct messages (`channel_type='direct'`, author != me). Top-channel list deferred — no denormalized unread count column yet.

All new queries in `getNavPanelData()` are single-row aggregates (count + coalesced sum) wrapped in per-sub-query `.catch()` fallbacks so a missing table can't take down the nav. `PanelCounterRow` accepts `number | string` for value so it can render '$12k', '4.5h', and '5d ago' without ugly casts.
userImpact: Three previously-blank rail panels now show actionable summaries — exactly what bookkeepers, attorneys, and team leads need to see at a glance without leaving the current screen.
riskNotes: Added queries are aggregates only, but if `chat_channel_members` rowcount grows large the team-chat unread query may need denormalization.
bullets: Billing panel: outstanding invoices + unbilled time/expenses + drafts
Trust panel: balance, reconciliation status, days-since-last-recon
Team Chat panel: unread @mentions + DMs (staff-only)
Per-sub-query .catch() prevents nav crash on missing data

summary: Removes env files from git tracking and tightens .gitignore — historical secrets in old commits should be considered leaked and rotated.
details:
Removes `.env.local`, `.env.vercel-check`, `.vercel/.env.development.local`, and `.vercel/.env.production.local` from git tracking. They remain on disk via `git rm --cached` so local development continues, but they will no longer be committed. Expands `.gitignore` to cover all `.env*` files with an explicit allowlist exception for `.env.example`.

**Critical follow-up.** Historical versions of these files that were committed before this change still exist in git history. Any real secrets in those historical versions should be considered leaked and rotated — check via `git log -p -- .env.local` etc. and rotate database URLs, API keys, service tokens, and auth secrets that show up.
userImpact: Dev workflow unchanged — local env files still load. Going forward, no risk of accidentally committing production credentials.
riskNotes: Past committed env files are still in git history. Audit and rotate any sensitive credentials that appeared in them.
bullets: Untracks all .env* files (kept on disk)
.gitignore now blocks all .env* except .env.example
Past commits still contain those files — rotate any leaked secrets

summary: Railway Buckets storage adapter (S3-compatible) plus a Chronicle Legal eFolder seed script with PHI sanitization.
details:
**Railway Buckets storage adapter.** Server-side adapter for Railway's native Buckets product (S3-compatible API at a tenant-specific endpoint, e.g. `https://t3.storageapi.dev`). Documents uploaded to the Railway bucket get stored with `railway://{bucket}/{key}` storage paths; `getDocumentSignedUrl()` routes those through a presigned URL minted via `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`. Supabase Storage remains the fallback for non-railway:// paths. Unlocks real document storage on staging — previously only Supabase Storage was configured for local dev. The bucket lives in the same Railway project as the staging Postgres so PHI stays inside a single trust boundary.

**Chronicle seed script.** `scripts/seed-from-chronicle.ts` (idempotent) transforms a Chronicle Legal eFolder scrape (saved under the gitignored `.local-seed/` sandbox) into 10 sanitized cases across all 5 SSD stages, ~200 document rows, and hearing calendar events. The sanitization layer replaces all identifying fields (names, SSNs, DOBs, addresses) with deterministic Faker-generated fakes; only the OCR'd PDF contents themselves are preserved unmodified so the AI extraction pipeline can be exercised end-to-end. Gated behind both a `--yes-staging` flag and `CONFIRM_PHI_STAGING=yes` env var to prevent accidental runs.

Document storage paths fall into two buckets: `railway://...` (real PDF, 30 docs) and `chronicle://...` (placeholder for metadata-only rows that don't yet have PDFs imported).

**New deps:** `@aws-sdk/client-s3`, `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`. **New env vars:** `RAILWAY_BUCKET_ENDPOINT`, `RAILWAY_BUCKET_REGION`, `RAILWAY_BUCKET_NAME`, `RAILWAY_BUCKET_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `RAILWAY_BUCKET_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`.
userImpact: The staging environment now has realistic data — 10 cases at every SSD stage with ~200 documents (30 with real PDFs) and hearing events. PHI is fully sanitized so it's safe to demo and develop against.
riskNotes: The Chronicle seed script is gated behind two flags but still loads real-format documents. Never run with non-sanitized inputs against any environment that could leak.
bullets: Railway Buckets S3 adapter wired into document URL routing
Idempotent Chronicle seed — 10 cases across 5 SSD stages
Faker-based PII sanitization (names, SSNs, DOBs, addresses)
Real OCR'd PDFs preserved for AI pipeline testing
Two-flag safety gate prevents accidental runs

summary: Multi-persona UX — per-role nav, role-specific landing pages, and an admin 'view-as' toggle that previews the app as any persona.
details:
Three-layer persona-aware UX driven by a single config file. Inspired by Salesforce's Lightning App model and Hogan Smith's persona briefs.

**Foundation.** `userRoleEnum` extended with `phi_sheet_writer` and `reviewer` (migration 0005, applied to staging). New `lib/personas/config.ts` defines `PERSONA_CONFIG` with label, defaultRoute, nav (filtered rail item IDs), workspaceDescription, primaryKpi for all 9 personas. New `lib/personas/effective-persona.ts` resolves the effective persona from session + `favorble_view_as_persona` cookie — cookie only honored when `actor.role === 'admin'`. Returns `{ actor, personaId, config, isViewingAs, actorPersonaId }`.

**Persona-aware navigation.** `two-tier-nav.tsx` filters rail items by `persona.config.nav`, preserving persona-defined ordering. Settings gear is gated on `actor.role === 'admin'`, not the previewed persona, so admins keep settings access while viewing as others. Root `/` redirects to the persona's `defaultRoute`. App shell layout calls `requireEffectivePersona()` and passes nav + admin flag down to TwoTierNav.

**View-as toggle (super-admin only).** `app/actions/view-as.ts` provides `setViewAsPersona` + `exitViewAs` server actions (both verify `actor.role === 'admin'`), write/delete the httpOnly `favorble_view_as_persona` cookie (sameSite lax, 8h, secure in prod), then redirect to the target default route. `view-as-menu.tsx` is a nested dropdown in the user menu listing all 9 personas. `view-as-banner.tsx` is a sticky amber banner ('Viewing as {label} — Preview only. Actions run as {actor name}') with an Exit button.

**Persona-aware /dashboard.** Server component that renders a welcome card, primary KPI card with a real DB query per persona (attorney hearings this week, case_manager open tasks, filing_agent ready-to-file, intake_agent new leads, mail_clerk inbound queue, MR within 30d, PHI assigned within 14d, reviewer 30d win rate, admin uses ERE credential count as cheap proxy), and a 4-up quick-link grid driven by `config.nav`. Every query wrapped in try/catch with em-dash fallback so the page never crashes.
userImpact: Each role sees only the navigation they need. Admins can preview the app as any persona via the 'View as' menu — useful for QA, training, and supporting users without screen-sharing.
riskNotes: The view-as cookie is httpOnly and admin-gated, but actions still run as the actor. Make sure mutations check the actor identity rather than the previewed persona for any role-sensitive operation.
bullets: 9 personas with role-specific nav + landing pages
Single config file drives every nav decision
Admin 'View as' toggle with sticky preview banner
Real per-persona KPIs on the dashboard
Migration 0005 adds phi_sheet_writer and reviewer roles

summary: Phase 4 wires up real interactive UIs for Billing, Trust, and Team Chat — replacing read-only views with working dialogs and live data flow.
details:
**Billing.** New server actions: `addInvoiceLineItem`, `addUnbilledTimeToInvoice`, `sendInvoice`, `getCasePicker`, `getClientPicker`, `recomputeInvoiceTotals`. New time entry dialog with case picker, duration parser ('1h 30m' / '90m' / '1.5h'), billable toggle, hourly rate, entry date. New invoice dialog with case/client pickers, due date, notes; routes to the invoice detail page on create. Invoice detail header actions: Send dialog (pre-fills `sentToEmail`), Mark Paid dialog (5 payment methods, defaults to outstanding balance), Download PDF disabled with 'Coming soon' tooltip. Invoice detail line-item management: manual Add Line Item dialog plus a conditional 'Import Unbilled Time' button (only when invoice has a `caseId`) that bulk imports billable time entries as line items and marks them billed. Edit actions disable when invoice status is paid or void.

**Trust.** Record Transaction dialog with account picker, deposit/withdrawal type, dollar→cent conversion, optional case picker, description, reference number, transaction date. Trust page fetches accounts + case picker in parallel.

**Team Chat.** Page rewritten as a thin server component that fetches real channels + messages (removed `MOCK_MESSAGES` fallback entirely). New `team-chat-client.tsx` with channel sidebar (grouped team vs case), active channel pane, optimistic message append, `getMessages` refetch on channel switch, auto-scroll on new messages, self-vs-others avatar styling, empty state when no channels exist.
userImpact: Billing finally works end-to-end — creating invoices, adding line items, importing time, sending, and marking paid. Trust transactions can be recorded with proper case linkage. Team chat shows real messages in real channels with optimistic send.
riskNotes: Dollar↔cent conversion happens at the dialog boundary — verify edge cases (very large amounts, fractional cents) before going to production billing.
bullets: Working New Invoice / New Time Entry dialogs with case pickers
Send + Mark Paid dialogs on invoice detail
'Import Unbilled Time' bulk imports billable hours
Trust Record Transaction dialog with cent precision
Team Chat now reads real channels + messages with optimistic send
